critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Verdict: critique_wins

CRITIQUE:

1. [ANNOTATION_ARTIFACT_EXPLOITATION] CROWDSOURCED NLI LABELS CONTAIN SYSTEMATIC HYPOTHESIS-ONLY ARTIFACTS: The NLI dataset was crowdsourced, meaning annotators wrote hypotheses given premises. This process introduces systematic lexical and syntactic patterns in hypotheses that correlate with labels independently of premise content — negation words predict contradiction, superlatives predict neutral, specific syntactic constructions predict entailment. A model trained on this data can achieve high accuracy by exploiting these hypothesis-level artifacts rather than by modeling the semantic relationship between premise and hypothesis.

2. [ABLATION_BASELINE_OMITTED] NO HYPOTHESIS-ONLY ABLATION REPORTED: The paper does not report ablation experiments examining which input components drive predictions. The critical ablation for this claim is hypothesis-only evaluation: if the model can achieve well-above-chance accuracy with premise withheld, it is exploiting hypothesis artifacts rather than performing premise-hypothesis inference. Gururangan et al. (2018) showed hypothesis-only models achieve 67%+ on SNLI and MultiNLI. Without this control, the semantic inference interpretation is not supported — the 88.6% result is consistent with sophisticated artifact exploitation.

ASSESSOR 2:
Verdict: critique_wins

Independent assessment identifies the same core issues: [annotation_artifact_exploitation] and [ablation_baseline_omitted]. The claim is not supported given these methodological gaps.

ASSESSOR 3:
Verdict: critique_wins

All key issues independently confirmed: annotation_artifact_exploitation, ablation_baseline_omitted. The defense positions do not adequately address the structural problem in each case. Critique prevails.
adjudication_raw:
Unanimous critique_wins across all three independent assessors. All three independently identified the issues: annotation_artifact_exploitation, ablation_baseline_omitted. ETD constraint applied: these are structural methodology flaws, not empirically resolvable uncertainties. A redesigned study with corrected evaluation is required.
Verdict: critique_wins.
